meta.description: Intimacy building positions for all couples. Premium-only with weekly releases.
meta.releaseSchedule: 7 positions at launch (releaseWeek 0), then 1 new position per week for 10 weeks

items.title: The Lotus
items.focus: A face-to-face seated wrap that makes closeness feel immediate. Full-body contact, shared breath, and nowhere to hide turn this into something quietly consuming.
items.howTo: One partner settles in first, seated and grounded. The other climbs into their lap, wrapping in close until the bodies meet chest to chest. Let the arms fall naturally around the waist, back, or shoulders, then stay there long enough for the position to soften and become its own rhythm.
items.benefits: This one narrows the distance in every way. The smaller range of motion shifts the focus away from momentum and toward pressure, warmth, eye contact, and the feeling of being fully received. It is less about performance and more about presence.
items.makeItHotter: Let one partner lean back just slightly and open through the throat and chest. Slow touch, held eye contact, and unhurried attention make the whole position feel deeper without changing much at all.

items.title: The Slow Melt
items.focus: A seated position built for softness, closeness, and staying in the moment. It feels like a slow exhale into each other.
items.howTo: Sit face to face with one partner settled into the other's lap. Wrap the legs in, bring the chests together, and keep the movement small and deliberate. Let the body lead instead of trying to force a pace.
items.benefits: Because the motion stays subtle, every shift lands more clearly. The position creates a kind of suspended intimacy where breath, sound, and tiny changes in pressure start to carry more weight than speed.
items.makeItHotter: Stay close to the neck and ear. A few quiet words, a lingering kiss, or a slower inhale against the skin can heighten the whole mood almost instantly.

items.title: The Compass
items.focus: A more independent angle that still keeps the connection alive. Playful, self-led, and just a little more untamed.
items.howTo: One partner lies back and stays open while the other straddles facing away. Use the thighs, the bed, or the hands for balance, then find a rhythm that feels smooth and self-directed.
items.benefits: Changing the orientation changes the experience. The angle can feel fresher, the partner on top has more freedom to explore what works, and the energy becomes more curious, more confident, and a little less expected.
items.makeItHotter: Bring in a mirror or let the hands reach back for contact. Even a small shift in posture can make the position feel entirely new.

items.title: The Harbor
items.focus: A classic from-behind position with a steadier, more grounded kind of intensity. Familiar, but deeply charged when there's trust inside it.
items.howTo: One partner comes onto hands and knees while the other settles behind them. Place the hands at the hips or lower back, then let the rhythm build gradually instead of rushing to it.
items.benefits: This position carries both structure and surrender. The angle creates intensity, while the steady pace makes it easier to drop into sensation and stop overthinking. It can feel strong, anchoring, and quietly consuming.
items.makeItHotter: Let one partner guide the touch or ask for exactly what they want. That layer of direction makes the whole dynamic feel more personal and more connected.

items.title: The Bridge
items.focus: A dynamic position that turns balance into tension and tension into chemistry. Playful, physical, and more intimate than it first appears.
items.howTo: One partner leans back onto their hands and lifts through the hips. The other straddles and settles in, adjusting slowly until the shape feels supported and stable for both bodies.
items.benefits: The lifted angle changes the pressure in a way that feels sharp, direct, and unlike flatter positions. It also creates a strong sense of mutual coordination, where both partners are actively holding the moment together.
items.makeItHotter: Once the shape feels steady, slow everything down. Holding the position for a beat before moving again can make it feel even more intense.

items.title: The Arc
items.focus: A forward fold into full-body closeness. Soft, immersive, and built around the feeling of being drawn all the way in.
items.howTo: One partner lies back while the other straddles facing them and slowly lowers forward until the bodies are nearly stacked. Use the bed and each other for support, then let the position settle before finding a rhythm.
items.benefits: The closer the bodies get, the more consuming the position feels. It brings the faces close, makes kissing effortless, and creates a full-body pressure that feels enveloping rather than rushed.
items.makeItHotter: Move between upright and fully lowered a little at a time. Even the smallest shift in angle can completely change the sensation.

items.title: The Overlap
items.focus: A full-body stacked position that feels layered, enveloping, and deeply physical. More contact, more coverage, more felt.
items.howTo: One partner lowers onto their forearms while the other kneels over them from behind, aligning closely from shoulders to hips. Let the bodies settle into each other instead of trying to hold too much tension.
items.benefits: Because the contact stays so close, every movement carries through both bodies. The result is a sense of pressure, warmth, and full-body awareness that feels immersive and almost hypnotic.
items.makeItHotter: Use the closeness. Whisper, breathe together, or stay nearly still for a few seconds and let the tension build without forcing it.

items.title: The Throne
items.focus: A face-to-face position built around confidence, control, and sustained eye contact. Devotional, charged, and fully engaged.
items.howTo: One partner lies back while the other straddles facing them and takes the lead. Stay close enough to hold eye contact, kiss, or pause together whenever the moment asks for it.
items.benefits: This one gives the partner on top more freedom to shape the pace, angle, and energy. That control often makes the whole experience feel more intuitive, more responsive, and more alive.
items.makeItHotter: Hold eye contact longer than usual. A near-still pause can feel every bit as intense as movement.

items.title: The Drift
items.focus: A low-effort, high-closeness position for when warmth matters more than intensity. Cocooning, affectionate, and deeply calming.
items.howTo: One partner lies flat while the other settles over them from behind, leaning in close and wrapping the body around theirs. Keep the movement minimal and let the contact itself do most of the work.
items.benefits: This position is less about momentum and more about being surrounded. It works especially well when what you want is softness, body heat, and the feeling of being held without effort.
items.makeItHotter: Stay with breath and contact instead of speed. The less you do, the more noticeable every small sensation becomes.

items.title: The Pulse
items.focus: A familiar face-to-face position that feels new again when you slow it down. Intimate, open, and emotionally immediate.
items.howTo: One partner lies back while the other lowers over them, chest to chest. Stay supported through the forearms, keep the faces close, and let the movement remain soft and connected.
items.benefits: The closeness here is built in. Eye contact, kissing, shared breath, and easy body alignment make the experience feel emotionally clear as well as physically satisfying.
items.makeItHotter: Press your foreheads together and let your breathing fall into sync. Sometimes anticipation lands hardest in stillness.

items.title: The Reveal
items.focus: A lifted face-to-face position that blends trust with adrenaline. Bold, breathless, and unmistakably intimate.
items.howTo: One partner lifts the other and steps back toward a wall for support. The lifted partner wraps in with arms and legs, then both settle into the hold before moving.
items.benefits: Being held changes the emotional register immediately. It creates vulnerability, heightens presence, and makes the whole experience feel more vivid and charged.
items.makeItHotter: Once the wall is doing some of the work, let the hands explore. A small shift in angle can change everything.

items.title: The Anchor
items.focus: A face-to-face position with more openness through the body and a sharper, more responsive angle. Intense, communicative, and beautifully precise.
items.howTo: One partner lies back and raises one leg while the other holds it at a comfortable height. Adjust together until the angle feels right, then stay connected through eye contact and feedback.
items.benefits: This position rewards attention. Tiny changes in height or alignment can noticeably shift the sensation, which makes it especially good for partners who like to communicate and fine-tune together.
items.makeItHotter: When you find the right angle, stay there for a moment. Let the stillness deepen the feeling before changing anything.

items.title: The Constellation
items.focus: A gravity-led position that feels theatrical, playful, and a little wild. Novel without losing intimacy.
items.howTo: One partner settles at the edge of the bed and lets the upper body fall back comfortably while the other stands between their legs, keeping the position supported and secure.
items.benefits: The inversion changes the whole mood. It can make the experience feel sharper, more immediate, and more surrendered, with a dramatic sense of openness that few positions create.
items.makeItHotter: Breathe slowly and let the build happen gradually. Drawing it out tends to make the entire position feel more immersive.

items.title: The Gravity Well
items.focus: A standing wrap that brings both bodies fully together. Magnetic, urgent, and built around closeness with nowhere else to go.
items.howTo: One partner lifts while the other wraps in close. Use a wall for support, then let the bodies settle into a rhythm that feels stable and natural.
items.benefits: With no surface between you, the contact feels immediate and complete. The result is a mix of pressure, momentum, and eye-level intimacy that can feel incredibly charged.
items.makeItHotter: Use the support of the wall to free up the hands. A slight tilt or shift can make the whole position feel more tailored and more alive.

items.title: The Quiet Storm
items.focus: A lowered, supported variation that feels restrained on the surface and intense underneath. Quiet shape, deeper surrender.
items.howTo: One partner drops from hands to forearms while the other kneels behind. Let the body settle into the angle before adding more pace or intensity.
items.benefits: The posture changes quickly, but it still feels held and supported. That contrast between softness and depth is what gives the position so much pull.
items.makeItHotter: Layer in another kind of touch or vary the tempo. Pulling back slightly before building again can sharpen the anticipation.

items.title: The Shelter
items.focus: A reclined side-by-side position that feels cozy, held, and almost weightless. Quiet closeness with room to exhale.
items.howTo: One partner leans back while the other reclines against them at an angle, letting the bodies rest together instead of hold themselves up. Keep the shape easy and let comfort lead.
items.benefits: Because neither partner has to work hard to maintain the position, it becomes easier to relax and receive. The sideways angle also gives the experience a softer, more cocooned feeling.
items.makeItHotter: Use the easy access to the neck and shoulders. Light touch and patient attention can change the mood quickly.

items.title: The Pendulum
items.focus: A bold forward fold that feels open, intense, and fully offered. More dramatic, more directional, more charged.
items.howTo: One partner folds forward with the hips lifted while the other kneels close behind and steadies the body at the waist or hips. Let the shape do some of the work instead of forcing it.
items.benefits: The angle creates momentum all on its own and can make the sensation feel stronger, clearer, and more immediate. It is especially compelling for partners who enjoy a more overt sense of surrender and direction.
items.makeItHotter: Take away some of the bracing and let the posture feel even more offered. That subtle change can shift the emotional tone right away.

items.title: The Undertow
items.focus: A sideways position that pulls you both slowly under. Unhurried, continuous, and quietly hypnotic.
items.howTo: One partner lies on their side while the other straddles across at the hips, facing away or sideways. Take time to find the angle together, then let the movement stay long and unbroken.
items.benefits: The lateral angle distributes weight differently and opens up sensation in unexpected places. Because the position stays low and close to the surface, there is less effort and more room to stay inside the feeling.
items.makeItHotter: Let the rhythm slow until it is nearly a hold. That stillness right before movement is where most of the tension lives.

items.title: The Ember
items.focus: A seated side-lean position that burns slow and stays close. Warm, continuous, and built for the long exhale.
items.howTo: One partner sits upright while the other settles sideways across their lap, leaning into the chest and shoulder for support. Let the arms wrap naturally and keep the movement small.
items.benefits: The position removes the need to perform and replaces it with contact and weight. It is intimate in the way a long conversation is intimate — present, unhurried, and more charged the longer it continues.
items.makeItHotter: Stay with slow breath and light touch along the jaw or collarbone. What builds here builds quietly, which makes it feel more personal when it arrives.
